title: The Acoustic Witness Vault
hypothesis: When archive records carry audible identity—tones that distort and decay with each access—the loss becomes visceral. Sound degradation communicates archival decay more immediately than visual wear alone, and the knowledge that closing the tab erases everything creates genuine urgency around preservation.
dataModel: Seeded radial records positioned along a spiral path. Each record stores: {id, text fragment, frequency (Hz), accessCount, degradation (0–1), locked (boolean), spiralIndex}. Records are generated from seed with variable text fragments drawn from witness testimony vocabulary.
recordCountStrategy: Seeded from hour + day-of-week: count = ((hour * 7 + dayOfWeek * 13 + 5) % 11) + 7, yielding 7–17 records. Deterministic per time-slot but never fixed at 18.
interactionModel: mouseenter/touchstart plays a brief quiet preview tone at the record's frequency. click accesses the record: increments accessCount, increases degradation (frequency shifts ±random, text characters corrupt). After 5 accesses, audio becomes noise. Long-press (1.5s) toggles lock: locked records are immune to degradation but play a dull flat tone and appear visually frozen. Drag repositions records along the spiral path, changing their temporal position and rewriting SVG connection paths. All state changes update sessionStorage and modify DOM classes, SVG path data, and Web Audio oscillator parameters.
stateModel: sessionStorage keyed by record ID. Each record persists: {text, frequency, degradation, locked, accessCount, spiralIndex}. On page load, state restores from sessionStorage. On tab close, everything vanishes—true ephemerality. Degradation formula: each click adds 0.15 + random(0.1), capping at 1.0. Text corruption replaces characters with ░▒▓ symbols proportional to degradation level.
visualLanguage: Spiral layout radiating from center (newest) to edge (oldest). SVG paths connect adjacent records with thin lines that fray as degradation increases. Records rendered as circular DOM nodes with text labels inside. Degraded records: corrupted text, dimmed opacity, irregular SVG edges. Locked records: solid border, muted colors, static appearance. Palette: aged paper (#e8dcc8), faded ink (#4a4540), rust (#8b4513), bone white (#f5f0e8). No glow, no particles, no radial light effects.
expectedStructuralChange: First draft to use Web Audio API as primary interaction channel rather than visual-only feedback. Introduces multi-sensory degradation (audio + text + visual). Moves from grid/table layouts to radial spiral arrangement. Uses sessionStorage instead of localStorage for true ephemeral records. Long-press mechanic adds temporal interaction dimension distinct from click/drag. Degradation is irreversible within session but vanishes completely on tab close, creating tension between preservation and inevitable loss.
